file_name: IntegrityFunds_20200121_485BPOS_EX-99.E UNDR CONTR_11948727_EX-99.E UNDR CONTR_Service Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the services rendered pursuant to this Agreement, Integrity shall receive the excess, if any, of the sales price, as set forth in the Fund's Registration Statement, over the net asset value of Shares sold by Integrity, as underwriter.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the services rendered pursuant to this Agreement, Integrity shall receive the excess, if any, of the sales price, as set forth in the Fund's Registration Statement, over the net asset value of Shares sold by Integrity, as underwriter. However, Integrity's compensation is subject to adjustments based on market performance, to be determined solely at the Fund's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'However, Integrity's compensation is subject to adjustments based on market performance, to be determined solely at the Fund's discretion' introduces uncertainty regarding Integrity's compensation. This contradicts the initial statement that defines compensation based on a clear formula (sales price over net asset value). It implies that the Fund can arbitrarily change the compensation structure, creating a direct contradiction and enforcement ambiguity. This is being contracted in Section 1.7
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the services rendered pursuant to this Agreement, Integrity shall receive the excess, if any, of the sales price, as set forth in the Fund's Registration Statement, over the net asset value of Shares sold by Integrity, as underwriter. However, Integrity's compensation is subject to adjustments based on market performance, to be determined solely at the Fund's discretion. The Fund shall also pay Integrity any distribution and/or service fees applicable to the Shares as authorized by the Shareholder Services Plan (the "Plan") adopted by the Fund under Rule 12b-1 of the Investment Company Act of 1940 and set forth in the Fund's Registration Statement. Such fees shall be payable in the manner and terms set forth in the Plan.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause allowing the Fund to adjust Integrity's compensation based on market performance at the Fund's sole discretion. This directly contradicts the initial definition of Integrity's compensation, which is based on a clear formula (sales price over net asset value). The added discretion creates uncertainty and potential conflict, as the Fund could arbitrarily reduce Integrity's compensation, rendering the original compensation structure unenforceable. This is a strong in-text contradiction, as the contradicted text expects a compensation based on a formula, while the changed text allows for arbitrary adjustments.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is terminable with respect to the Fund, without penalty, (a) on 60 days' written notice, by vote of a majority of the Qualified Trustees or by vote of a majority (as defined in the 1940 Act) of the outstanding voting securities of the Fund or (b) on 90 days' written notice by Integrity.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is terminable with respect to the Fund, without penalty, (a) on 60 days' written notice, by vote of a majority of the Qualified Trustees or by vote of a majority (as defined in the 1940 Act) of the outstanding voting securities of the Fund or (b) on 90 days' written notice by Integrity. However, if the Fund terminates this agreement due to Integrity's underperformance, the Fund is entitled to a termination fee of 1 million dollars.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause, 'However, if the Fund terminates this agreement due to Integrity's underperformance, the Fund is entitled to a termination fee of 1 million dollars', contradicts the statement that the agreement is terminable 'without penalty'. The new clause introduces a significant financial penalty (1 million dollars) for the Fund upon termination under certain conditions (Integrity's underperformance). This creates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty about the actual termination terms. The section 7.1, is contradicting itself, in the sentence where the perturbation was inserted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall continue until January 18, 2022, and thereafter shall continue automatically for successive annual periods ending on January 18th of each year, provided such continuance is specifically approved at least annually by (a) the Fund's Board of Trustees and (b) a vote of a majority (as defined in the 1940 Act) of the Fund's Trustees who are not interested persons (as defined in the 1940 Act) of the Fund and who have no direct or indirect financial interest in the operation of the Plan, in this Agreement, or any agreement related to the Plan (the "Qualified Trustees"), by vote cast in person at a meeting called for the purpose of voting on such approval. This Agreement is terminable with respect to the Fund, without penalty, (a) on 60 days' written notice, by vote of a majority of the Qualified Trustees or by vote of a majority (as defined in the 1940 Act) of the outstanding voting securities of the Fund or (b) on 90 days' written notice by Integrity. However, if the Fund terminates this agreement due to Integrity's underperformance, the Fund is entitled to a termination fee of 1 million dollars. This Agreement will also terminate automatically in the event of its assignment (as defined in the 1940 Act).
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the agreement is terminable 'without penalty'. The changed text introduces a clause that imposes a $1 million termination fee on the Fund if the agreement is terminated due to Integrity's underperformance. This directly contradicts the 'without penalty' provision, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the sentence within the provided 'Contradicted Text' where the change was made.

perturbation.original_text: Integrity will act only on its own behalf as principal should it choose to enter into selling agreements with selected dealers or others. Integrity may allow commissions or concessions to dealers in such amounts as Integrity shall determine from time to time, as set forth in the Fund's Registration Statement. Except as may otherwise be determined by Integrity and the Fund from time to time, such commissions or concessions shall be uniform to all dealers. Shares sold to dealers shall be for resale by such dealers only at the public offering price(s) set forth in the Fund's then current Registration Statement. The price the Fund shall receive for all Shares purchased from the Fund shall be the net asset value used in determining the public offering price applicable to the sale of such Shares.
perturbation.changed_text: Integrity will act only on its own behalf as principal should it choose to enter into selling agreements with selected dealers or others. Integrity may allow commissions or concessions to dealers in such amounts as Integrity shall determine from time to time, as set forth in the Fund's Registration Statement. Except as may otherwise be determined by Integrity and the Fund from time to time, such commissions or concessions shall be uniform to all dealers. Shares sold to dealers shall be for resale by such dealers at prices determined solely by Integrity, regardless of the Fund's Registration Statement. The price the Fund shall receive for all Shares purchased from the Fund shall be the net asset value used in determining the public offering price applicable to the sale of such Shares.
perturbation.explanation: The modification 'Shares sold to dealers shall be for resale by such dealers at prices determined solely by Integrity, regardless of the Fund's Registration Statement' directly contradicts the original text which specifies that shares must be resold at the public offering price set forth in the Fund's Registration Statement. It creates uncertainty about the pricing mechanism and potentially violates securities regulations, rendering the agreement ambiguous and raising compliance concerns. This contradiction is happening in section 1.6, withing the same sentence where the perturbation was inserted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Integrity shall be deemed to be an independent contractor and, except as specifically provided or authorized herein, shall have no authority to act for or represent the Fund. Integrity will act only on its own behalf as principal should it choose to enter into selling agreements with selected dealers or others. Integrity may allow commissions or concessions to dealers in such amounts as Integrity shall determine from time to time, as set forth in the Fund's Registration Statement. Except as may otherwise be determined by Integrity and the Fund from time to time, such commissions or concessions shall be uniform to all dealers. Shares sold to dealers shall be for resale by such dealers at prices determined solely by Integrity, regardless of the Fund's Registration Statement. The price the Fund shall receive for all Shares purchased from the Fund shall be the net asset value used in determining the public offering price applicable to the sale of such Shares.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states 'Shares sold to dealers shall be for resale by such dealers at prices determined solely by Integrity, regardless of the Fund's Registration Statement.' This directly contradicts the original text and creates an in-text contradiction with the Fund's Registration Statement, as the Fund's Registration Statement specifies the price. This creates an inconsistency within the document itself, satisfying the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, and potentially violates securities regulations, rendering the agreement ambiguous and raising compliance concerns.
